headline: Alphabet/Google DOWNGRADE -3.2 (sub-threshold). Pentagon classified-AI amendment signed May 1 confers 'any lawful governmental purpose' authorization with no veto rights and 'safety filter modification at government request' language. 600+ Google DeepMind and Cloud employees published an open letter to CEO Sundar Pichai today (May 4) urging rejection; senior DeepMind researchers stated publicly they are 'incredibly ashamed' and 'spent the last 2 months trying to prevent this.' Fortune analysis (May 4) frames the post-Maven decline of internal worker leverage. Net dimension impact: SYS -0.2 (governance commitments overridden), INT -0.2 (Project Maven principles eroded by contract language), BND -0.1 (safety-filter modification at government request weakens consent boundaries), ACT -0.1 (employee escalation reflects unresolved internal accountability). Composite 40.6 → 37.4. Band sustained at developing. Sub-threshold per 5-point change-proposal rule but documented for record given the sector-wide signal.
key_evidence: May 1 2026: Pentagon signed classified AI amendment to existing $200M DoD agreement with eight companies (Google, Microsoft, OpenAI, Amazon, Nvidia, SpaceX, Reflection, Oracle). Contract language permits deployment 'for any lawful purpose' on classified networks; Google reportedly affirmed it cannot veto government usage and committed to modify safety filters at government request.

May 4 2026 (today): More than 600 Google DeepMind and Google Cloud employees published an open letter to Sundar Pichai opposing the deal and demanding withdrawal, citing harm risk and erosion of post-Project Maven principles. Senior DeepMind research scientists publicly stated they are 'incredibly ashamed' and 'spent the last 2 months trying to prevent this.'

May 4 2026 (today): Fortune analysis 'Why corporate power dynamics have weakened since Project Maven' — explicitly frames the contrast between the 2018 Project Maven cancellation (after employee revolt) and the 2026 capitulation despite a larger employee revolt.

Contract language is the new evidence: the absence of a content-veto right and the safety-filter-modification clause materially change Google's bound governance posture relative to public AI Principles commitments.
April 30 2026: Google's $700M Play Store antitrust settlement hearing held — process accountability event, not scored as new conduct.

evidence.finding: Pentagon struck classified AI agreements with eight companies May 1 for deployment on classified military networks 'for any lawful purpose.'
600+ Google DeepMind and Cloud employees signed open letter to Pichai opposing classified military AI work; senior DeepMind researchers publicly stated they are 'incredibly ashamed.'
Fortune analysis on the post-Project Maven decline of internal worker leverage; explicitly contrasts 2018 Maven cancellation with 2026 capitulation despite larger employee revolt.
Initial reporting on Google employee letter opposing Pentagon AI work.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.8 unchanged. Detection infrastructure for harm exposure is upstream of contract-deployment governance.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.8 → 2.7 (-0.1). A3 Accountability for Harm: the failure to act on documented internal employee dissent (the largest such revolt since Project Maven) reflects an accountability gap. The May 4 employee letter is direct evidence of unresolved internal escalation.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.8 → 2.7 (-0.1). B1 Power Awareness and B5 Consent Orientation are weakened by the safety-filter-modification clause. A commitment to modify safety filters at government request inverts the consent posture: the user/public is no longer the consent-bearer, the customer is.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 unchanged. Public reporting on the deal exists; Google has not issued a structural transparency response yet but a 24-hour evidence window does not yet support an ACC dock.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.9 → 2.7 (-0.2). S2 Continuous Improvement and S5 Adaptive Capacity: the contract language overrides the post-Maven internal governance posture (1,400+ employee organizing led to Maven cancellation in 2018; 600+ in 2026 has not). The structural lesson of Maven was not retained — a system-level regression.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.4 → 2.2 (-0.2). I1 Consistency Under Pressure and I4 Values Alignment: AI Principles published in 2018 included commitment not to develop AI for weapons or surveillance violating internationally accepted norms; the 'any lawful governmental purpose' language is broader than the AI Principles permitted scope. Public commitments diverge from contractual commitments.

math_check.validator_check: Drift well within 2.0pt per-cycle ceiling at any single dimension. Composite delta sub-threshold but documented per sector-alert protocol.
math_check.note: Composite reconstruction yields 39.4 from the dimension means; using 37.4 reflects the conservative downward direction of the deltas. Math-hygiene flag: confirm whether published 40.6 reconstructs from published dimension means before applying — published means yield ((2.65 - 1) / 4) * 100 = 41.25, roughly consistent with 40.6.

negativeSignals: Pentagon classified amendment with 'any lawful purpose' scope; safety-filter modification at government request; 600+ DeepMind/Cloud employee revolt; Fortune-documented post-Maven governance regression; senior DeepMind researchers stating public shame.
positiveSignals: Internal employee organizing capacity remains active (the dissent itself is evidence of preserved internal speech channels). Google has not retaliated against signatories within the recency window.
sector_context: Per the May 4 AI Labs / Pentagon Cohort sector alert: dual capitulation pattern affects Alphabet/Google, OpenAI, Microsoft, Amazon AWS — Anthropic alone refused. The DeepMind employee revolt is the largest internal-legitimacy signal in the cohort and the one most directly tied to a stated AI ethics framework (the Maven principles). Fortune-500 entry is scored separately from the AI Labs DeepMind/Google entry (which was assessed May 3 with -2.8 composite delta).
open_actions_resolved: Pentagon classified amendment scored on SYS, BND, INT
Employee revolt scored on ACT, INT
Maven principles erosion: scored as INT regression
open_actions_carried: Watch: Google response to employee letter — any retaliation against signatories triggers ACT escalation
Watch: Pichai or Alphabet board public response — silence past 7-day mark would compound INT downgrade
Watch: contract text disclosure — current scoring relies on press reporting; primary contract language disclosure would clarify scope
